name: Arizona v. Inter Tribal Council of Arizona, Inc.
summary: The Court held that the NVRA preempted Arizona's requirement that voters provide documentary proof of citizenship when registering using the federal form, affirming federal supremacy in setting voter registration standards.
significance: This case addressed the scope of federal preemption of state voter registration requirements under the National Voter Registration Act and the Elections Clause, which is directly relevant to the preemption analysis here.
